key_insight: Conforming finite-element matrices can be treated as locally Gram-representable SPD systems, enabling a black-box multilevel algebraic preconditioner built from element-wise SPSD energy splits and coarse Gram operators.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical AMG-like route for difficult solid-mechanics and PDE discretizations, especially when standard AMG struggles with high-order, anisotropic, or vector-valued FEM systems.
method: Define a cover of degrees of freedom and check for a local Gram/SPSD splitting of the SPD matrix.
Build local spectral problems from the Gram factors to construct interpolation P.
Form coarse operators via Galerkin coarsening with G_c = G P and A_c = G_c^T G_c.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong robustness on multiple challenging FEM test cases, including grad-div in H(div), hyperdiffusion in H^2, and linear elasticity, where classical AMG needed more than 10^5 iterations and LS-AMG-DD often achieved 2–5 orders of magnitude smaller errors at the same residual tolerance.
watch_item: The approach depends on obtaining suitable local Gram factors for element blocks, so practical setup and factorization choices may affect robustness and implementation cost.
paper_title: A black-box, multilevel algebraic preconditioning framework for conforming finite elements
